articles.title: IOS 27 LEAK REVEALS NEW SIRI DESIGN, CAMERA APP, MORE
articles.summary: Bloomberg's new leaked illustrations of iOS 27 reveal Apple's biggest Siri redesign yet, with the assistant moving into the Dynamic Island and gaining a new “Search or Ask” interface that combines voice commands, typed requests, web search, and chatbot-style conversations. The update will reportedly let users launch apps, send messages, search personal data, and access AI-powered results through rich interactive cards, while also supporting third-party AI services like ChatGPT, Gemini, and Claude.

articles.title: ROKU CHANGES HOMESCREEN WITH MAJOR OVERHAUL
articles.summary: Roku is overhauling its homescreen for the first time in over a decade to drive more engagement through personalized content recommendations and improved app discovery. The new interface will prominently feature users' most-used apps, include "top picks for you" recommendations, and adapt to different household members' viewing habits. The update aims to maintain Roku's simplicity while making the platform smarter and more useful for its 100 million-plus users.

articles.title: GOOGLE WALLET AND ITS REDESIGN WERE SUPPOSED TO IMPROVE THE USER EXPERIENCE, BUT A LOT OF PEOPLE ARE FURIOUS. WHY?
articles.summary: Google redesigned its Wallet app to improve user experience by adding colors, new buttons, and better organization of ID cards like passports and driver's licenses. Many users are frustrated with the changes, complaining about poor readability due to smaller fonts, a confusing layout with two cards displayed side by side, and a hidden search feature.

articles.title: I'M MAKING A SPEC FOR DESIGN SYSTEM DOCUMENTATION
WHY I DON'T USE ICON FONTS IN FIGMA
articles.summary: Icon fonts in Figma require a cumbersome 7-step process that involves leaving the application to browse icons on external websites and copying tiny renderings. Users must memorize exact icon names without visual previews, creating a poor experience compared to SVG components with fuzzy search and instance-swapping capabilities. Icon fonts also make comparing different glyphs tedious since icons can't be easily placed side by side for visual comparison.

articles.title: THE MCDONALD'S CHARITY LOGO FINALLY GOT THE REDESIGN IT DESPERATELY NEEDED
articles.summary: Ronald McDonald House Charities has introduced a long-overdue rebrand that replaces its outdated logo featuring Ronald McDonald's hand with a warmer, more modern and versatile identity better suited to its global mission of supporting families with ill children. The redesign keeps the charity's emotional warmth and connection to McDonald's through color and hand-drawn elements, while improving clarity, donor appeal, and independence as the organization expands toward its goal of doubling the number of families it serves by 2030.

articles.title: AI MARKETING EMAIL BUILDER
articles.summary: Bluor is an AI-powered email marketing tool that helps users create professional marketing emails. The platform uses artificial intelligence to automate the email-building process. It simplifies email marketing for businesses and marketers.

articles.title: TINY ENGINE FOR 3D VOXEL SCENES
articles.summary: heerich.js is a minimalist JavaScript engine that constructs 3D voxel compositions and distills them into pristine SVGs.

articles.title: DESIGNING WHERE THE PIXELS ACTUALLY LIVE
articles.summary: A senior product designer at Slack describes how AI tooling helped her bridge the longstanding gap between static design mockups and real, interactive product experiences. Working primarily in Cursor, she now builds and deploys functional prototypes that teammates can actually test, enabling faster alignment and richer design conversations. The transition came with trade-offs — AI occasionally breaks existing work and demands smaller, more deliberate iteration steps — but the ability to design closer to the real experience feels like a meaningful shift.

articles.title: WHY UX SHOULD OWN RETENTION
articles.summary: UX teams should own customer retention since acquiring new customers costs five times more than keeping existing ones, yet most organizations focus on acquisition while neglecting retention strategy. Retention currently falls between departments with no clear ownership, creating an opportunity for UX to claim this strategic responsibility and tie their work directly to revenue metrics. By owning retention, UX can shift from being perceived as making screens look nice to being seen as strategically important for business outcomes like lifetime customer value and churn reduction.

articles.title: THE REVOLUTION WILL NOT BE OPTIMISED: ON FRICTIONMAXXING AND THE CREATIVE VIRTUES OF INCONVENIENCE
articles.summary: Modern life is increasingly designed to eliminate friction and inconvenience, but in doing so it may also be removing the pauses, boredom, and small inefficiencies that foster creativity, intimacy, reflection, and human connection. The growing “frictionmaxxing” trend argues that some forms of slowness and inconvenience — like walking to a shop, cooking, waiting, or doing things manually — are not obstacles to life but essential parts of experiencing it fully, pushing back against a culture obsessed with constant optimisation and productivity.

articles.title: PRINCIPLES OF LOGO DESIGN BY GEORGE BOKHUA
articles.summary: George Bokhua's Principles of Logo Design is a 224-page practical guide that teaches designers to create timeless logos using geometric grids and systematic approaches rather than following trends.

articles.title: THESE TYPEFACES COULD BE RUINING YOUR BRAND'S DESIGNS. HERE'S WHY
articles.summary: A web design studio analyzed common typefaces and revealed its "ten most-hated fonts" that could damage brand designs, including popular choices like Arial, Tahoma, and Times New Roman.
